mission.title: Install and Configure Container2wasm on Kubernetes
mission.description: container2wasm is a tool that converts container images into WebAssembly (WASM) format, enabling them to run on WASI runtimes or in the browser. Installing this tool allows you to leverage existing containerized applications in a lightweight, portable format suitable for various environments.

steps.title: Add the container2wasm Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the container2wasm Helm repository to your local Helm setup. This will allow you to install the container2wasm chart easily.
```bash
helm repo add container2wasm https://ktock.github.io/container2wasm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install container2wasm using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install the container2wasm chart into your Kubernetes cluster. This command will create a new namespace called 'container2wasm' and deploy the application there.
```bash
helm install container2wasm container2wasm/container2wasm --namespace container2wasm --create-namespace --version 0.8.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the container2wasm pods are running successfully in the 'container2wasm' namespace. This command will list all the pods in the specified namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace container2wasm
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the container2wasm deployment. Edit the deployment to set CPU and memory limits.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment container2wasm --namespace container2wasm
# In the editor, add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Test the container2wasm functionality
steps.description:
After installation, you can test the functionality of container2wasm by converting a sample container image to WASM. Use the following command to convert an Ubuntu image:
```bash
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> --namespace container2wasm -- c2w ubuntu:22.04 out.wasm
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the container2wasm pods running in the 'container2wasm' namespace, and you will be able to convert container images to WASM format. You can verify the functionality by executing the conversion command and checking for the output file.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add container2wasm https://ktock.github.io/container2wasm
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment container2wasm --namespace container2wasm
# In the editor, add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> --namespace container2wasm -- c2w ubuntu:22.04 out.wasm

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
